user_input: I get 'Disk quota exceeded' writing to my project area. How do I get more space?
reference: The project is at its storage limit. Clear unneeded data or request a quota increase; large intermediate files can go in the scratch area, which is not quota-limited.
reference_contexts: The project is at its storage limit. Clear unneeded data or request a quota increase; large intermediate files can go in the scratch area, which is not quota-limited.

user_input: Output files my job wrote are missing on the login node. Where did they go?
reference: They were likely written to node-local /tmp, which is cleared when the job ends. Write outputs to your project or scratch directory so they persist.
reference_contexts: They were likely written to node-local /tmp, which is cleared when the job ends. Write outputs to your project or scratch directory so they persist.

user_input: Reading millions of small files is extremely slow. Is the filesystem broken?
reference: Many tiny files are an anti-pattern on parallel filesystems. Pack them into larger archives (tar/HDF5) or stage them to local scratch at job start for much better throughput.
reference_contexts: Many tiny files are an anti-pattern on parallel filesystems. Pack them into larger archives (tar/HDF5) or stage them to local scratch at job start for much better throughput.

user_input: Which partition should I use for a memory-heavy workload?
reference: Use a high-memory (-himem) partition for memory-bound work; standard partitions suit normal runs. Per-node memory figures are in the platform documentation.
reference_contexts: Use a high-memory (-himem) partition for memory-bound work; standard partitions suit normal runs. Per-node memory figures are in the platform documentation.

user_input: Which is the most recent CPU partition and how do I benefit from it?
reference: Use the newest CPU partition in service and rebuild your code with an architecture-tuned flag to exploit its instruction set.
reference_contexts: Use the newest CPU partition in service and rebuild your code with an architecture-tuned flag to exploit its instruction set.

user_input: How do I pick a specific compiler and MPI version rather than the defaults?
reference: Defaults are shown by 'module list'. Use 'module avail' to see versions and 'module load name/version' to pin a specific one.
reference_contexts: Defaults are shown by 'module list'. Use 'module avail' to see versions and 'module load name/version' to pin a specific one.
